channel.title: SpotPulse Trending
channel.description: Live web pulse — India & Global intelligence dashboard.

item.title: Assam swearing-in LIVE: Himanta Biswa Sarma takes oath as Assam CM for second consecutive term
item.description: Second to take the oath as Minister designate is BJP's Rameswar Teli, a former Union Minister and two-time MP from Dibrugarh

item.title: Gold Rate Today, May 12: Check 18, 22 and 24 carat gold prices in Chennai, Mumbai, Delhi, Kolkata and other cities
SP MP Ajendra Singh Lodhi booked over ‘anti-national’ remark on PM Modi
item.description: The police action came at the complaint of BJP district media in-charge Satyendra Pratap Gupta, who accused Mr. Lodhi of stoking “caste hatred” with his remark on Mr. Modi

item.title: Vodafone Idea shares drop 4% after telco clarifies on treasury stock transfer report. Here's what it said
item.description: Shares of Vodafone Idea fell nearly 4% after the company clarified it had not received any communication from Vodafone Group regarding a reported stake transfer proposal. The clarification followed an 8% rally triggered by reports suggesting Vodafone Plc may transfer part of its stake to strengthen Vodafone Idea’s balance sheet and fundraising efforts.

item.title: Tamil Nadu politics LIVE: Velumani-Shanmugam-led AIADMK faction extends support to ruling TVK
item.description: Ahead of crucial floor test, split wide open in AIADMK

item.title: Google identifies first AI-developed zero-day exploit - Notebookcheck
item.description: Google identifies first AI-developed zero-day exploit Notebookcheck

item.title: South Korea’s radical 'AI dividend' proposal: A bold future or a market nightmare?
item.description: South Korea is discussing sharing the wealth generated by artificial intelligence. A government official suggested distributing AI profits, causing a stir in the stock market. Companies like Samsung and SK Hynix are seeing huge profits from AI chips. This move highlights concerns about wealth concentration and calls for broader economic benefits for citizens.

item.title: Malaysia, Tamil Nadu share deep ties, says PM Anwar Ibrahim; congratulates new Tamil Nadu CM Joseph Vijay
item.description: In a post on X, Anwar Ibrahim said that for many years, CM Vijay's "adoring fans watched him defeat corrupt politicians and assorted villains within three hours, usually after a short interval"

item.title: ‘Cancel your own foreign tours’: Opposition leaders in Maharashtra slam PM
item.description: Mr Modi has appealed citizens to avoid foreign travel for a year, avoid buying gold, use public transport and electric vehicles [EVs], and adopt work from home

item.title: India's IT index falls to three-year low on weak earnings outlook, demand worries - MSN
item.description: India's IT index falls to three-year low on weak earnings outlook, demand worries MSN

item.title: Vodafone Sales Beat as Strategic Pivot to Main Markets Pays Off
item.description: Vodafone Group Plc reported organic revenue growth last quarter that beat analysts’ expectations as the operator accelerates a pivot to focus on its biggest markets in Germany and the UK.

item.title: Defence, Energy, Diaspora: Why PM Modi’s UAE Visit Matters Now More Than Ever
item.description: With global oil markets under strain because of the West Asia conflict, India is looking to secure dependable supply chains and protect itself from future disruptions.

item.title: TVK MLA-Elect Won By 1 Vote. Now DMK Rival's Challenge, Trust Vote Red Light
item.description: Vijay's party swept to a historic win in last month's election, picking up 108 (down to 107 when Vijay quits one of the two seats he won) of the state's 234 seats.

item.title: Who is Nandini Harinath? All about Indian scientist whose saree was displayed at US museum
item.description: A saree worn by Indian space scientist Nandini Harinath during a key moment in ISRO’s historic Mars Orbiter Mission has found a place at the Smithsonian’s National Air and Space Museum in Washington, DC.

item.title: Simca Advertising IPO GMP signals 17% listing pop: Check subscription and other details on Day 3 of the offer
item.description: Simca Advertising IPO GMP today is ₹31 as against ₹30 on Monday and ₹14 a day before that, signalling growing investor interest in the counter. At the current GMP, Simca Advertising IPO listing price could be ₹214, suggesting a listing pop of 16.9%.

item.title: Mumbai water cut from May 15: BMC announces 10% reduction as reservoir levels drop to 23%
item.description: BMC has announced a 10 per cent water cut across Mumbai from May 15, with reservoir levels at just 23 per cent of the city's annual requirement. The cut will also affect Thane and Bhiwandi-Nizampur

item.title: Why is stock market crashing today? Sensex drops over 800 points - Rs 5 lakh crore investors wealth wiped - The Times of India
item.description: Why is stock market crashing today? Sensex drops over 800 points - Rs 5 lakh crore investors wealth wiped The Times of India

item.title: Recode Studios share price hits 5% upper circuit after bumper debut
item.description: Recode Studios shares debuted strongly on BSE SME, opening at ₹213.10, 35% above the issue price. The stock reached an upper circuit limit of ₹223.75. The IPO raised ₹44.6 crore, with a subscription rate of 234.64 times.

item.title: Sony upgraded its wearable AC so it’s cooler and better at hugging your neck
item.description: Sony has announced a new version of the wearable personal air conditioner the company first revealed in 2019 ahead of the 2020 Tokyo Olympics. The latest version is an upgrade to last year's Pro model, and while the new Reon Pocket Pro Plus isn't a complete overhaul of the hardware, it comes with performance upgrades […]

item.title: DNR, local fire crews battle wildfire in Presque Isle County - dailypress.net
item.description: DNR, local fire crews battle wildfire in Presque Isle County dailypress.net

item.title: ADNOC Gas: Ready to Export LNG As Soon As Hormuz Reopens
item.description: ADNOC Gas reported resilient 1Q earnings, with net income beating analyst estimates despite disruption to exports through the Strait of Hormuz. The company is also managing the impact of recent incidents at its Habshan facility, with plans to reach 80% capacity by the end of 2026. Peter van Driel, CFO of ADNOC Gas spoke to Bloomberg’s Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche on the disruptions caused

item.title: Iran war oil shortage forces Japan snack giant to use black-and-white packaging
item.description: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in ink Japan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade. Calbee said on Tuesday 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. Cont

item.title: Zerodha's Nikhil Kamath backs gold-based stablecoin over dollar-linked crypto; says UPI saved India from a bigger risk
item.description: Kamath proposes a gold-backed stablecoin for India to unlock value from idle gold, criticizing the push for dollar-linked digital assets. He lauds India's cautious regulatory stance on cryptocurrencies, suggesting that blockchain could help monetize the vast gold reserves held by households.

item.title: PM Modi’s call to stop foreign travel comes as spending in this area has already been falling
item.description: RBI data shows that the real growth areas in terms of foreign spending are purchases of immovable assets abroad and investments in foreign debt and equity

item.title: "Speculative, Misleading": Pak On Report Claiming It Is Shielding Iran Jets From US Strikes
item.description: The report claimed that Pakistan allowed Iranian military planes to use its airfields as parking.

item.title: India Weighs Emergency Steps to Protect Forex Reserves | Insight with Haslinda Amin 05/12/2026
item.description: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Stock market crash: ₹11 lakh crore wiped out in 4 sessions; should investors follow Warren Buffett and hold cash?
item.description: As the stock market faces significant declines, investors are left wondering whether to follow Warren Buffett’s strategy of holding cash. With geopolitical tensions and market volatility rising, will staying on the sidelines prove to be the wisest move for investors?

item.title: Paschi Beats on Profit as CEO Focuses on Mediobanca Integration
item.description: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA’s profit in the first quarter beat expectations, giving tailwind to Chief Executive Officer Luigi Lovaglio and his effort to integrate Mediobanca SpA.

item.title: Groww turns multi-bagger for Peak XV, YC, Ribbit after IPO lock-in expiry
item.description: Groww's early investors have sold shares after their IPO lock-in period ended. This move has generated massive returns for firms like Peak XV, Ribbit Capital, and Y Combinator. The block deal involved millions of shares. Groww's stock has seen significant growth since its listing.

item.title: IMD forecasts rain and gusty winds across North India; yellow alert issued for Delhi
Korea Floats ‘Citizen Dividend’ from AI Gain
item.description: A top South Korean policymaker said the nation should pay citizens a “dividend” using taxes on AI profits, underscoring growing pressure to redistribute gains from a boom that’s enriched chipmakers like Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. Bloomberg's Sangmi Cha breaks down what we know. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Dollar pressure, PM’s appeal may shift Indian tourists towards domestic travel: Atul Thakkar
item.description: Indian outbound travel may see a temporary slowdown due to rising currency pressures and recent government remarks, prompting travellers to reconsider overseas holidays. Experts anticipate behavioural shifts rather than regulatory action, with a potential pivot towards more affordable ASEAN destinations. AI is also poised to disrupt travel aggregators, shifting consumer behaviour towards direct bookings.

item.title: Not Missiles, But Energy Resilience: The Unexpected Winners Of Iran War
item.description: Nations underwent an unplanned stress test when disruption in the Strait of Hormuz rattled global trade.

item.title: OpenAI, Microsoft agree to cap revenue-sharing at $38 billion: Report
item.description: The development comes ‌as OpenAI and Microsoft renegotiated a contract last month

item.title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Falls 800 Points After Trump Says Ceasefire On Life Support
item.description: Sensex Today, Stock Market, Nifty, Share Market Live News Updates: Markets to open in red.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Investors Track War Stalemate and Stock Moves
item.description: Gold was little changed following a modest two-day gain, as investors assessed the deadlock in the Middle East conflict, and swings in Asian stock markets ahead of a key US inflation report.

item.title: Airstrikes, drone attacks claim dozens of lives in Sudan: UN - IBTimes India
item.description: Airstrikes, drone attacks claim dozens of lives in Sudan: UN IBTimes India

item.title: CBSE 12th Result 2026 News Live: Scorecard Releasing Today? DigiLocker Hints 'Coming Soon'
item.description: CBSE 12th result login details should be the same as mentioned on the CBSE admit card 2026.

item.title: Seychelles calls for urgent UN Security Council reform and supports initiative on veto restrictions at Nairobi ministerial meeting - Seychelles News Agency
item.description: Seychelles calls for urgent UN Security Council reform and supports initiative on veto restrictions at Nairobi ministerial meeting Seychelles News Agency

item.title: Uniper’s Trading Arm Returns to Profit as Gas Business Improves
item.description: Uniper SE’s trading division was back in profit in the first quarter as its gas division performed better and wasn’t impacted by supply disruptions related to the war in the Middle East.
